content: Self-Refine’s lightweight, no-training iterative output polishing and Reflexion’s dynamic memory-augmented self-reflection agent framework can be combined to form a two-layer hierarchical self-improvement architecture for LLMs: Self-Refine handles local, step-by-step refinement of individual outputs, while Reflexion accumulates cross-task feedback from all Self-Refine iterations in its dynamic memory to update global problem-solving heuristics. This architecture retains Self-Refine’s core advantage of requiring no additional training or supervision, while solving its key limitation of failing to accumulate long-term improvement across tasks.
